First Demon World.notes: Shipped as a free update alongside DAIMA Character Pack 2 (PS5/Xbox/PC Sep 22-25 2025); added to Switch/Switch 2 in the May 26 2026 update.
First Demon World.summary: DAIMA Demon-World arena; the only stage added by a DLC-era free update. Map ID 0020.
sources: research/06-pve-dlc-unlocks.md §4 (DAIMA Pack 2 + First Demon World free stage)
First Demon World.body: First Demon World is the DRAGON BALL DAIMA arena added as a **free update alongside DAIMA Character Pack 2** (Sept 22-25 2025 on PS5/Xbox/PC; rolled out to Switch and Switch 2 with the May 26 2026 update). Like the other open battle arenas it has destructible terrain (blasts carrying the `Map Destruction` flag damage the environment). It is the only new stage delivered during the Season Pass 1 DLC window; the next batch (Kami's Palace, Stratosphere/Planet Vegeta and two more) is tied to the unreleased Super Limit-Breaking NEO expansion.

Planet Namek.notes: Default Episode-Battle and Versus arena (Namek arc). A stage-locked 'Power of Namek' item grants +2% all damage here (excludes Destroyed Planet Namek).
Planet Namek.summary: Open destructible arena; the canonical Namek battlefield (map ID 0000).
sources: research/04-mechanics-frame-data.md §9 (Map Destruction flag; Power of Namek item)
Planet Namek.body: Planet Namek is an open arena with destructible terrain — blasts flagged `Map Destruction` carve up the environment. It is one of the maps tied to a stage-locked item buff: 'Power of Namek' grants +2% all damage when fighting here (notably it does **not** apply on the separate Destroyed Planet Namek stage), which implies the game tracks these as discrete map IDs.

Destroyed Planet Namek.notes: The collapsing/exploding Namek variant. The 'Power of Namek' item buff does NOT apply here (only on intact Planet Namek).
sources: research/04-mechanics-frame-data.md §9 (Power of Namek excludes Destroyed Namek)
Destroyed Planet Namek.body: Destroyed Planet Namek is the late-arc collapsing version of the Namek battlefield. Research notes it is treated as a distinct map from intact Planet Namek: the 'Power of Namek' +2% damage item explicitly excludes it.

City.notes: Urban arena. Covered by the 'Power of Earth' item buff family (which excludes City Ruins and the Hyperbolic Time Chamber).
City.summary: Destructible urban battlefield with multiple time-of-day variants (map IDs 0003/0053/0103/0203).
sources: research/04-mechanics-frame-data.md §9 (Power of Earth item; City Ruins/HTC excluded)
City.body: City is a destructible urban arena offered in several time-of-day variants. It falls under the 'Power of Earth' +2% damage item set — research notes that buff family specifically **excludes City Ruins and the Hyperbolic Time Chamber**, implying City proper is treated as a standard Earth map.

City Ruins.notes: Ruined-city arena. Explicitly EXCLUDED from the 'Power of Earth' damage item (unlike the standard City stage).
sources: research/04-mechanics-frame-data.md §9 (Power of Earth excludes City Ruins)
City Ruins.body: City Ruins is the rubble-strewn city arena. Research flags it as a distinct map: the 'Power of Earth' +2% damage item that applies on ordinary Earth stages does **not** apply on City Ruins.

Hyperbolic Time Chamber.notes: The featureless white infinite void. Excluded from the 'Power of Earth' damage item; effectively a flat, hazard-free arena with no destructible terrain.
Hyperbolic Time Chamber.summary: Endless white training void; flat, no environmental destruction (map ID 0006).
sources: research/04-mechanics-frame-data.md §9 (Power of Earth excludes Hyperbolic Time Chamber)
Hyperbolic Time Chamber.body: The Hyperbolic Time Chamber is the endless white void from the Cell/Buu training arcs — a flat, featureless arena with no destructible structures. Research notes it is excluded from the 'Power of Earth' damage item that applies on normal Earth maps, consistent with it being a neutral, hazard-free space.

World Tournament.notes: Ring-based arena. Ring-out-style ultimates trigger a 'World Tournament Evacuation' flag (crowd/ring evacuation) per datamined blast flags.
World Tournament.summary: World Martial Arts Tournament ring; supports ring-out evacuation ultimates (map IDs 0004/0204).
World Tournament.body: The World Tournament stage is the ring-based arena from the World Martial Arts Tournament. Certain ring-out-style ultimate attacks carry a datamined `World Tournament Evacuation` flag, which forces a crowd/ring evacuation sequence — a stage-specific interaction unique to the tournament maps.

Tournament of Power Arena.notes: Universe Survival arena (Tournament of Power). Open battlefield with destructible terrain.
Tournament of Power Arena.summary: Tournament of Power battleground from the Super arc; map ID 0005.
Tournament of Power Arena.body: The Tournament of Power Arena is the multi-universe battleground from Dragon Ball Super's Universe Survival arc. It is an open arena with destructible terrain consistent with the datamined `Map Destruction` blast flags.
